Bead bookkeeping: assign SandyOx to raptorq golden test issue,
update timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support scene discovery and activation for common automation workflows.

## Scope
- List scenes.
- Activate a scene.
- Optional: create/update/delete scenes (dangerous; defer unless needed).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.scenes.read`
- `ha.scenes.activate` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `ha.scenes.write` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Scene activation is a side effect:
  - receipts + audit events include scene id + action

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.scene.list`
  - `ha.scene.activate`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - activate flow
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Scenes can be activated safely and auditable.

Tests passing. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:22:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a0x","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core entity state access:
- read current state/attributes
- perform controlled state writes where supported

## Scope
### Read
- Get entity state by entity_id.
- Bulk list states (bounded).

### Write (domain-limited)
- For entities that support direct state writes (e.g., input_* helpers), allow setting state.
- For device control (lights/switches/etc), prefer service calls (`flywheel_connectors-3ef`).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.entities.read`
- `ha.entities.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Sensitive attributes (location, camera URLs, etc.) must be redacted from logs.
- Writes emit receipts + audit events.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.entity.get`
  - `ha.entity.list`
  - `ha.entity.set_state` (domain-limited)

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - state parsing
  - redaction rules
  - write gating
  - receipts/audit for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Entity access is reliable, bounded, and privacy-preserving.

All with tests. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:22:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a1j","title":"Implement Discord gateway session resumption","description":"Implement session resumption for the Discord gateway. When disconnected, the connector should attempt to resume using session_id and sequence number rather than starting a fresh session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T14:38:27.440747757Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:20:58.252868388Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:20:58.252868388Z","close_reason":"Implemented Discord gateway session resumption: GatewayResume type, conditional Resume/Identify sending, RESUMED event handling, session state management methods (update_session, update_sequence, clear_session, can_resume), resume_url usage for reconnection","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","title":"Six golden erasure recovery tests fail in fcp-raptorq/src/golden.rs. Decoder produces data not matching original payload. 723 other raptorq tests pass. Also causes 2 downstream failures in fcp-store repair integration. Acceptance: all 6 golden tests and fcp-store repair tests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:04:31.034109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-26T22:27:35.484144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decoder","raptorq","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2033,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak investigation 2026-03-26: Traced the failure path. Encoder produces source ESIs 0..K-1 and repair ESIs K'..K'+R-1 (K' >= K per RFC 6330). Decoder try_reconstruct() classifies received symbols: ESI<K as source (identity equation), ESI>=K as repair (calls repair_equation_rfc6330). Gap K..K' is filled with zero-padded virtual symbols. Basic roundtrip tests PASS (723), only erasure recovery tests FAIL (6 golden + 2 store). encode_store_reconstruct also fails even with all symbols present, suggesting the decoder produces corrupt output under certain symbol count/size combinations. The BTreeMap change (uncommitted at time of testing) is NOT the cause — failures reproduce on the committed HashMap version. Root cause likely in InactivationDecoder Gaussian elimination or the constraint/repair equation wiring. Needs deep codec-level investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:27:35Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","title":"Six golden erasure recovery tests fail in fcp-raptorq/src/golden.rs. Decoder produces data not matching original payload. 723 other raptorq tests pass. Also causes 2 downstream failures in fcp-store repair integration. Acceptance: all 6 golden tests and fcp-store repair tests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SandyOx","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:04:31.034109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:30:17.346840Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decoder","raptorq","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2033,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak investigation 2026-03-26: Traced the failure path. Encoder produces source ESIs 0..K-1 and repair ESIs K'..K'+R-1 (K' >= K per RFC 6330). Decoder try_reconstruct() classifies received symbols: ESI<K as source (identity equation), ESI>=K as repair (calls repair_equation_rfc6330). Gap K..K' is filled with zero-padded virtual symbols. Basic roundtrip tests PASS (723), only erasure recovery tests FAIL (6 golden + 2 store). encode_store_reconstruct also fails even with all symbols present, suggesting the decoder produces corrupt output under certain symbol count/size combinations. The BTreeMap change (uncommitted at time of testing) is NOT the cause — failures reproduce on the committed HashMap version. Root cause likely in InactivationDecoder Gaussian elimination or the constraint/repair equation wiring. Needs deep codec-level investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:27:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","title":"fcp.spotify - Spotify Connector (Music & Podcast Platform)","description":"# fcp.spotify - Spotify Music & Podcast Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Plan and implement an FCP2-compliant Spotify connector for playback/library/search workflows with correct OAuth provisioning and strict single-zone isolation.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 72/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (Media/Entertainment family).

**Why Critical**: Spotify is the **largest music streaming platform** (500M+ users). Enables agents to control playback, manage playlists, discover content, and integrate music into productivity workflows. Unique capability for ambient/focus automation.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (Media + Personal Productivity)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 4 Advanced (weeks 13+)

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Playback Control
- Play/pause/skip tracks
- Control volume
- Manage queue
- Device selection

### Library Management
- Search music and podcasts
- Create/edit playlists
- Save to library
- Follow artists

### Discovery & Recommendations
- Get recommendations
- Browse new releases
- Access personalized playlists
- Artist/album exploration

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Play some focus music"
"Add this song to my workout playlist"
"What's currently playing?"
"Skip to the next track"
"Find podcasts about AI"
"Create a playlist for deep work"
"Show me new releases from followed artists"
"Lower the volume to 30%"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Home Assistant Integration
**Scene-based audio:**
- Music as part of home scenes
- Time-based playback automation
- Presence-aware music

### Calendar Integration
- Focus music during work blocks
- Different playlists for meeting types
- Podcast queue for commute times

### Agent Mail Integration
- Music recommendations → agent threads
- Playlist collaboration
- Content discovery sharing

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- Index podcast transcripts
- Search music by mood/genre
- Contextual recommendations

### AI Provider Integration
- Generate playlist descriptions
- Mood analysis for recommendations
- Podcast summarization

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Web API** — Full Spotify functionality
- **OAuth 2.0** — User authorization required
- **Scopes** — Granular permission model

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `oauth2` — OAuth handling
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Key Scopes
| Scope | Access |
|-------|--------|
| `user-read-playback-state` | Current playback |
| `user-modify-playback-state` | Control playback |
| `playlist-modify-public` | Edit playlists |
| `user-library-read` | Read saved items |
| `user-read-recently-played` | Listening history |

### Resource Model
```
User
├── Playlists
├── Saved Tracks
├── Followed Artists
└── Devices

Track → Album → Artist
Playlist → Tracks
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:media:spotify:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:media:spotify:playback` | Playback only | Control, no library changes |
| `z:media:spotify:readonly` | Read-only | Browse and search |
| `z:media:spotify:playlists` | Playlist management | Playlist CRUD |

### Data Sensitivity
- Listening history reveals preferences
- Playlist names may be personal
- Generally low sensitivity overall

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.spotify.com:443`
- `accounts.spotify.com:443` (OAuth)

### Credential Injection
- OAuth tokens via `CredentialId`
- Refresh tokens for long-lived access
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `spotify.playback.read` — Current playback state
- `spotify.library.read` — Saved items
- `spotify.playlists.read` — User playlists
- `spotify.search` — Search catalog

### Write Operations
- `spotify.playback.control` — Play/pause/skip
- `spotify.queue.add` — Add to queue
- `spotify.playlists.write` — Create/edit playlists
- `spotify.library.write` — Save/remove items

### Administrative
- `spotify.playlists.delete` — Delete playlists
- `spotify.follow.manage` — Follow/unfollow

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get Current Playback
```json
GET /v1/me/player
```

Response:
```json
{
  "device": {"id": "abc123", "name": "Living Room Speaker"},
  "is_playing": true,
  "item": {
    "name": "Track Name",
    "artists": [{"name": "Artist Name"}],
    "album": {"name": "Album Name"}
  },
  "progress_ms": 45000
}
```

### Start/Resume Playback
```json
PUT /v1/me/player/play
{
  "context_uri": "spotify:playlist:37i9dQZF1DX5trt9i14X7j",
  "offset": {"position": 0}
}
```

### Search
```json
GET /v1/search?q=focus+music&type=playlist&limit=10
```

### Create Playlist
```json
POST /v1/users/{user_id}/playlists
{
  "name": "Deep Work Focus",
  "description": "Music for concentration",
  "public": false
}
```

### Add to Playlist
```json
POST /v1/playlists/{playlist_id}/tracks
{
  "uris": ["spotify:track:abc123", "spotify:track:def456"]
}
```

---

## Productivity Patterns

### Focus Music Automation
```
Agent receives "starting focus session"
→ Search for "focus" playlists
→ Start playback on preferred device
→ Set appropriate volume
→ Queue additional tracks
```

### Context-Aware Playback
- Morning: Energetic playlist
- Work: Focus/concentration music
- Break: Upbeat/casual
- Evening: Relaxing/ambient

### Podcast Integration
- Queue podcasts for commute
- Resume where left off
- Track listening progress

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- OAuth flow handling
- Search query construction
- Playback command formatting
- Playlist operations

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Multi-device scenarios
- OAuth refresh handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Scope enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Spotify calls in unit tests
- Playback control works
- Search functional
- Playlist CRUD operational
- Device selection works
- OAuth refresh implemented

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":851,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Spotify connector verified: 161 tests (86 unit + 75 integration), all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:56:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.1","title":"fcp.spotify: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Spotify connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** under FCP2.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (search/browse metadata)
  - `operational` (library mgmt, playback control)
  - `streaming` (player state as an event stream; polling-backed)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin Spotify Web API host(s)
  - explicitly pin any image/media download hosts if used
- State model declaration:
  - OAuth refresh state (stored by platform, not connector)
  - player polling cursor/state (if persisted)

## Acceptance criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.

FCP2 format with: archetypes (knowledge, operational, streaming), full operation list with schemas, capability families, NetworkConstraints (api.spotify.com + i.scdn.co host_allow), state model. Verified by SunnyMoose.","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:19:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.2","title":"fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)

## Goal
Automate Spotify onboarding with minimal human steps while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE via provisioning recipes.
- Scope selection:
  - read-only scopes for search/history
  - separate scopes/capabilities for playback control and library writes
- Device readiness checks:
  - verify the account has at least one available playback device (when required)
  - handle “Premium required” cases explicitly
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Mockable in CI.
